L. 110–351section 671 of this title

children.text: shall be coordinated with other State or local agencies that promote service coordination or provide information and referral services, including the entities that provide 2–1–1 or 3–1–1 information systems where available, to avoid duplication or fragmentation of services to kinship care families;

shall be planned and operated in consultation with kinship caregivers and organizations representing them, youth raised by kinship caregivers, relevant government agencies, and relevant community-based or faith-based organizations;

children.label: A
children.text: each other;

children.label: B
children.text: eligibility and enrollment information for Federal, State, and local benefits;

children.label: C
children.text: relevant training to assist kinship caregivers in caregiving and in obtaining benefits and services;

children.label: D
children.text: relevant legal assistance and help in obtaining legal services; and

children.label: E
children.text: connections to individualized assistance, as needed;
shall provide outreach to kinship care families, including by establishing, distributing, and updating a kinship care website, or other relevant guides or outreach materials;

shall promote partnerships between public and private agencies, including schools, community based or faith-based organizations, and relevant government agencies, to increase their knowledge of the needs of kinship care families and other individuals who are willing and able to be foster parents for children in foster care under the responsibility of the State who are themselves parents to promote better services for those families;
may establish and support a kinship care ombudsman with authority to intervene and help kinship caregivers access services; and
may support any other activities designed to assist kinship caregivers in obtaining benefits and services to improve their caregiving.
a description of how the grant will be used to implement the activities described in subsection (a);

a description of the types of children and families to be served, including how the children and families will be identified and recruited, and an initial projection of the number of children and families to be served;

a description of how the entity will directly fund, or provide data to the Secretary for, an evaluation which will publish and submit information to the clearinghouse described in and which is designed to meet the requirements of , or a description of how the funds will be used to help the State transition to a program for which the State will seek reimbursement under ;section 676(d)(2) of this titlesection 671(e)(4)(C) of this titlesection 674(a)(7) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: documentation of support from the relevant local or State child welfare agency; or

children.label: B
children.text: a description of how the organization plans to coordinate its services and activities with those offered by the relevant local or State child welfare agency;
an assurance that the entity will cooperate fully with any evaluation provided for by the Secretary under this section; and

children.label: A
children.text: documentation of support from a relevant community-based organization with experience serving kinship families when applicable; or

children.label: B
children.text: a description of how the organization plans to coordinate its services and activities with those offered by the relevant community-based organizations.

children.heading: Grant duration
children.label: 1 Grant duration
children.text: The Secretary may award a grant under this section for a period of not less than 1 year and not more than 3 years.

children.heading: Number of new grantees per year
children.label: 2 Number of new grantees per year
children.text: The Secretary may not award a grant under this section to more than 30 new grantees each fiscal year.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: d Federal share
children.text: An entity to which a grant is made under this section may use the grant to pay not more than 75 percent of the cost of the activities to be carried out by the entity pursuant to this section.

children.heading: Form of grantee contribution
children.label: e Form of grantee contribution
children.text: A grantee under this section may provide not more than 50 percent of the amount which the grantee is required to expend to carry out the activities for which a grant is awarded under this section in kind, fairly evaluated, including plant, equipment, or services.

children.heading: Use of grant
children.label: f Use of grant
children.text: A grantee under this section shall use the grant in accordance with the approved application for the grant.

children.heading: Reservation of funds for technical assistance
children.label: g Reservation of funds for technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary may reserve 2 percent of the funds made available under for each fiscal year to provide technical assistance to recipients of grants under this section.section 629g(b)(6) of this title
